questions.question: What is the primary function of the physical layer in the OSI model?
expectedPoints: To define the physical means of transmitting data between devices.

questions.question: Describe the difference between guided media and unguided media.
expectedPoints: Guided media uses a physical path to transmit data, while unguided media does not.
Examples of guided media include copper cables and fiber optic cables.
Examples of unguided media include wireless media.

questions.question: What is packet switching?
expectedPoints: A technique that breaks data into small packets and forwards them between devices.

questions.question: Describe the difference between connectionless packet switching and connection-oriented packet switching.
expectedPoints: Connectionless packet switching does not establish a connection between devices before data is transmitted, while connection-oriented packet switching does.
Examples of connectionless packet switching include UDP and IP.
Examples of connection-oriented packet switching include TCP and ATM.

questions.question: What is the OSI model?
expectedPoints: A seven-layer model that consists of the application layer, presentation layer, session layer, transport layer, network layer, data link layer, and physical layer.
